gaps: A petition-disposition prior that separates historical opinion-bearing SCOTUS records from modern certiorari-stage petitions would improve calibration for sparse snapshots.
Snapshot-side cluster metadata or a short procedural summary would reduce ambiguity without requiring agents to fetch new case facts.
helpful: fedcourts paths identified the materialized event and snapshot paths.
fedcourts stats provided SCOTUS petition-disposition base rates.
fedcourts query helped compare resolved SCOTUS petition rows at a high level.
notes: The snapshot was too sparse for a confident case-specific disposition call, so the forecast relied on the visible cluster signal and local resolved-event base rates.
